title: Startups Ditch Databases
dek: Digital-native startups are ditching rigid databases for their agentic stacks
meta_description: Digital-native startups ditch rigid databases for agentic stacks, AI law startup Norm raises $120M
body_html:
As the startup world continues to evolve, one of the biggest developments of the day is the shift away from traditional relational databases. Digital-native startups are ditching rigid databases for their agentic stacks, citing the need for more flexibility and scalability. According to VentureBeat, this move is driven by the limitations of legacy infrastructure in supporting AI models and agents.

Agentic Era Bottlenecks

The gap between what AI models and agents can produce and what legacy infrastructure can reliably support is known as architectural drag, and it is the defining bottleneck of the agentic era. The data layer underneath an agentic system must handle variable schemas, vector embeddings, real-time retrieval, and multi-tenant scale, often simultaneously and without human intervention to manage migrations.

Traditional relational databases weren't natively designed for document flexibility or AI capabilities. Fixed schemas require manual updates every time an AI agent introduces a new data shape, while separate vector databases add latency. As a result, startups are looking for alternative solutions that can keep up with the demands of their agentic stacks.

For example, NoSQL databases have become a popular choice for startups looking to ditch traditional relational databases. These databases offer more flexibility and scalability, making them better suited to handle the demands of agentic systems. As the startup world continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more companies making the switch to alternative database solutions.

As VentureBeat notes, the move away from traditional relational databases is a key development in the startup world. As startups continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with AI and agentic systems, they'll need databases that can keep up. This shift has significant implications for the future of startup development and the role of database technology in supporting agentic stacks.

AI Law Startup Norm Raises $120M

AI law startup Norm has raised a $120 million Series C round led by Khosla Ventures, valuing the startup at $1.2 billion. According to TechCrunch, this funding round is a significant milestone for the company, which is working to apply AI to the legal industry.

The funding will be used to further develop Norm's AI-powered legal tools, which aim to make the legal process more efficient and accessible. As the legal industry continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more startups like Norm applying AI to traditional legal processes.

Norm's funding round is also a sign of the growing interest in AI law startups. As AI continues to transform industries, it's likely that we'll see more startups emerging in this space. With its significant funding and valuation, Norm is well-positioned to be a leader in the AI law space.

As TechCrunch notes, Norm's funding round is a significant development in the startup world. The company's use of AI to transform the legal industry has the potential to make a major impact, and its funding will help to further develop its AI-powered legal tools.

Anthropic Brings Claude Cowork to Mobile and Web

Anthropic has launched Claude Cowork on mobile and web, expanding a tool that has quietly become the company's bridge between the developer-centric world of AI coding agents and the far larger market of knowledge workers who never open a terminal. According to VentureBeat, this rollout marks a strategic inflection for Anthropic.

The rollout, which begins in beta with Max subscribers before expanding to additional plans, transforms Cowork from a desktop-only agent into a cross-device platform where tasks can start on a laptop, continue on a mobile device, and be completed on the web. This expansion has significant implications for the future of AI and its application in the workplace.

As VentureBeat notes, the launch of Claude Cowork on mobile and web is a key development in the startup world. The tool has the potential to make AI more accessible to a wider range of users, and its expansion to mobile and web is a significant step in that direction.

The rollout of Claude Cowork on mobile and web is also a sign of the growing importance of cross-device platforms in the startup world. As users increasingly expect to be able to access tools and services across multiple devices, startups like Anthropic are responding by developing cross-device platforms that meet this need.

Savi’s App Aims to Protect Consumers from AI Scams

The company Savi has raised $7 million in seed funding and is launching its app for iPhone and Android to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom. According to TechCrunch, this app is a significant development in the startup world, as it aims to address the growing problem of AI-powered scams.

The app uses AI to detect and prevent scams, and it has the potential to make a major impact in the fight against AI-powered fraud. As AI continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more startups emerging in this space, working to develop AI-powered solutions to protect consumers from scams.

As TechCrunch notes, the launch of Savi's app is a key development in the startup world. The app has the potential to make a major impact in the fight against AI-powered scams, and its launch is a significant step in that direction.

The growing problem of AI-powered scams is a significant concern for consumers, and startups like Savi are working to develop AI-powered solutions to address this issue. As AI continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more startups emerging in this space, working to protect consumers from AI-powered fraud.

Bidbus Lets Dealerships Bid on Used Cars

Bidbus, which lets dealerships bid on used cars, has raised $15 million in a Series A round that was led by early-stage mobility fund Ibex Investors. According to TechCrunch, this funding round is a significant milestone for the company, which is working to transform the used car market.

The funding will be used to further develop Bidbus's online bidding platform, which aims to make the process of buying and selling used cars more efficient and transparent. As the used car market continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more startups like Bidbus emerging, working to apply AI and other technologies to the industry.

As TechCrunch notes, Bidbus's funding round is a key development in the startup world. The company's use of online bidding to transform the used car market has the potential to make a major impact, and its funding will help to further develop its online platform.

The used car market is a significant industry, and startups like Bidbus are working to apply AI and other technologies to make it more efficient and transparent. As AI continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more startups emerging in this space, working to transform the used car market.

The bottom line

In conclusion, the startup world is undergoing significant changes, driven by the adoption of AI and other technologies. From the shift away from traditional relational databases to the emergence of new startups in the AI law and used car spaces, it's clear that the startup world is evolving rapidly.

  • The shift away from traditional relational databases is a key development in the startup world, driven by the need for more flexibility and scalability in agentic stacks.
  • The emergence of AI law startups like Norm has the potential to transform the legal industry, and its funding round is a significant milestone in this space.
  • The launch of Claude Cowork on mobile and web is a significant development in the startup world, as it aims to make AI more accessible to a wider range of users.
  • The growing problem of AI-powered scams is a significant concern for consumers, and startups like Savi are working to develop AI-powered solutions to address this issue.
  • The used car market is a significant industry, and startups like Bidbus are working to apply AI and other technologies to make it more efficient and transparent.
headline: Digital-native startups are ditching rigid databases for their agentic
